text:
UNIVERSITY OF BOUMERDES M’HAMED BOUGARA 
FACULTY OF SCIENCES, DEPARTMENT OF COMPUTER SCIENCE 
Specialty: L3-SI / Module: AM/ Responsible: Mr. BITIT 
 
Page 1 sur 1 
 
 
 Mini-project: UMBB MobGuide 
 
The objective of this work is to develop a mobile application using the Android Studio 
environment. The proposed application, titled UMBB MobGuide, serves as a digital guide for the 
University of Boumerdès. This application aims to provide clear information regarding the 
university's various faculties and departments.  
UMBB MobGuide offers a set of features allowing users to easily access information 
related to the University of Boumerdès. Among the main features, we can mention : 
 
University Presentation: Display a general description of UMBB. 
 
Faculty Consultation: View a list of the university's faculties. 
 
Department Consultation: Access departments associated with each faculty. 
 
Detailed View: Consult detailed information for a faculty or department (name, description, 
specialties, etc.). 
 
Contact & Location: Options to contact a faculty or department via SMS, email, or phone 
call, and locate them using Google Maps. 
 
Search (Optional): Search for a specific faculty or department. 
 
Multilingual Interface (Optional): Allow users to choose between English and Arabic. 
For the storage and display of the application's data, use dynamic arrays (ArrayList) and ListViews. 
 
It is requested to:  
1. Develop the application (using Android Studio, Java/XML).  
2. Produce a report (6 pages maximum) describing: the different application components 
(classes, interfaces, etc.), the distribution of tasks among group members, and the features 
implemented as well as those not implemented.  
3. Produce a video with a maximum duration of 10 minutes, illustrating the application, its 
main features, and its user interface.  
4. Create a folder on Google Drive in which the report, source code, and presentation 
video will be deposited. This folder must then be shared with the instructor using the email 
address: "umbb.mobguide2026@gmail.com".  
 
Important Remakes : 
- 
This project is to be completed in groups of 4 to 6 students. 
- 
Similar works or any form of cheating will be severely sanctioned. 
- 
The deadline for submitting the source code, report, and presentation video will be set 
later (one week before classes end). 
- 
The names of all participating students must be clearly and completely mentioned.
